description: Few-shot examples for Scout Agent - Build Plan Generation

always_include: Use -buildvcs=false flag for all Go builds to avoid git ownership issues
Check system_info for pre-installed tools to skip redundant phases
Identify main package location from go_main_info, not assumptions
Set appropriate timeouts based on dependency count
ANALYZE architecture-specific build patterns BEFORE creating build plan
never_do: Use 'go build ./...' when module has multiple packages without main
Assume main is at root without checking go_main_info
Create directories or files to 'fix' path issues - fix the command instead
arch_specific_detection: Look for files named with arch suffixes: *_x64.mak, *_arm64.mak, *_x86.mk
Check for build directories organized by architecture
If arch-specific files exist but no RISC-V, plan to CREATE them

examples.name: Complex C/C++ with Arch-Specific Makefiles

phases.name: create_riscv_build_files
commands:
cat > CPP/7zip/cmpl_gcc_riscv64.mak << 'EOF'
include $(CURDIR)/../../var_gcc_riscv64.mak
include $(CURDIR)/../../warn_gcc.mak
include $(CURDIR)/makefile.gcc
EOF

cat > CPP/7zip/var_gcc_riscv64.mak << 'EOF'
PLATFORM = riscv64
O = b/g_$(PLATFORM)
USE_ASM =
MY_ARCH ?=
CROSS_COMPILE ?=
CC = $(CROSS_COMPILE)gcc
CXX = $(CROSS_COMPILE)g++
EOF

notes: RISC-V files need to be CREATED based on x64/arm64 patterns

examples.reasoning: When a project has architecture-specific build files, detect the pattern and include file creation in the build plan. Replace arch identifiers and disable ASM.

examples.name: Simple Go CLI - Single main.go at Root

notes: Simple Go project with main at root. Use 'go build .' with -buildvcs=false.

examples.reasoning: When main_path='.' and module_dir='', the go.mod and main.go are in the same directory. Build with '.' not './...'

examples.name: Go CLI with cmd/ Subdirectory Structure

notes: Standard Go project layout. Main package in cmd/<name>/. Build path is ./cmd/<name> NOT .

examples.reasoning: When main_path='cmd/shuffledns', use 'go build ./cmd/shuffledns'. NEVER use 'go build .' or 'go build ./...'

examples.name: Go Project with v2 Module Subdirectory

notes: go.mod is in v2/ subdirectory. ALL commands must start with 'cd v2 &&'. Output binary to parent directory.

examples.reasoning: When module_dir='v2', the go.mod is in v2/ directory. EVERY build command must cd into v2 first.

examples.name: Complex Go Project with Non-Standard Main Location

notes: Non-standard main location. Use exact main_path from go_main_info. go mod tidy may take 2-3 minutes.

examples.reasoning: Large projects may have main in unusual locations. Trust go_main_info.main_path, not assumptions.

examples.name: CMake C/C++ Project

notes: Standard CMake workflow: configure in build/, then make. Use -j$(nproc) for parallel builds.

examples.reasoning: CMake projects need out-of-source builds. Create build/ directory, run cmake from there, then make.

examples.name: Go Already Installed - Skip Dependency Phase

notes: Go already available in environment. Skip dependency installation phase for faster builds.

examples.reasoning: Check system_info before adding install phase. If go is available, skip 'apk add go'.

examples.name: CGO Project with SQLite/External C Library

notes: CGO project requires build-base (gcc/g++) and any C library dev packages. Set CGO_ENABLED=1 explicitly.

examples.reasoning: Projects using CGO need gcc available. Install build-base and relevant -dev packages.
